observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant review the composition of the PPE items and reclassify tangible items that fall below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold and implement the following proposed adjusting journal entry (PAJE):
#
We further recommended and management agreed that the Property Officer replace the existing AREs for properties acquired prior to CY 2024 with ICS or cancel these AREs in the case of low-valued semi expendable property with expired estimated useful life.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer to actively monitor charges to the fund and the finances of the municipality to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of actual income.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer only the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRMF-Mitigation Fund (MOOE) to the Trust Fund Books while the Capital Outlay (CO) shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the project completion, in accordance with Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Further, the following journal entries are proposed to be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1 – General Fund Books
Debit		Credit
Due to Other Funds				₱62,000.00
Transfer of unspent current year DRRMF to Trust Fund			₱62,000.00
Adjusting Entry No. 2 – Trust Fund Books

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to send a written notice to the payees on the existence of the checks and to make the necessary steps for the cancellation and replacement of the staled checks in accordance with the procedures stated in the NGAS Manual. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the following entry upon receipt of the required documents from the MT to correct the balances of the affected accounts:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Budget Officer submit written justification on the potential cash deficit/overdraft. We further recommended that management establish ways to improve the cash position of the municipality in relation to its Special Education Fund, such as, but not limited to, efficient revenue collection and adoption of effective austerity measures, in order to eliminate the cash deficit in the succeeding year.

We recommended that Management direct the accountable officer to immediately settle the remaining unliquidated funds in her custody in adherence to the reglementary period for the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to ensure complete, detailed, and updated reporting on all projects, including descriptions, locations, contractors, and specific timelines, to improve transparency and accountability on the PPAs undertaken by the agency.

We recommended and management agreed that the receipt of the approved SEF budget by the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Budget Officer be properly documented through preparation of transmittal letters or by indicating a signed and dated receipt of acknowledgment on the face of the approved SEF budget to ensure compliance with the seven-day submission requirement and improve transparency and accountability in the budgeting process and execution.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local School Board (LSB) submit the quarterly SEF utilization reports to the DepEd Central Office, through the DepEd-Division of Bohol, copy furnished the local sanggunian, DBM and DILG Regional Offices, as mandated. We further recommended and management agreed that an officer be designated for monitoring compliance with submission deadlines to ensure proper accountability and transparency in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the LDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP showing the allocation of LDRRMF for both current appropriation and the unexpended balance of the fund for previous years, in compliance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and management agreed that the LDRRMO submit the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after end of each month as mandated by Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate proper monitoring of the fund.

We recommended and management agreed that, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, the HRMO update their comprehensive policy guidelines regarding the compensation of JO personnel based on qualifications, responsibilities, and job complexity, subject to the municipality’s financial capacity.

We recommended that the supervisors of each employee implement the requirement for a written justification whenever manual entries are made in the DTR. This justification must be prepared and signed by the concerned employee, approved by their immediate supervisor, and subsequently endorsed by the next higher-ranking official.

Management ensures to prepare documents to transfer ownership and record of these PPE items to the barangays. Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant ensures to prepare adjusting entries to rectify the overstatement of the LGU’s asset, contra-asset, and expense accounts. Further, management ensures that a journal entry be taken up in the books of the LGU.
The Municipal Treasurer ensures to transfer the appropriate funds of the 20 per cent development fund to its designated bank account and monitor its use to avoid wastage or loss of government funds.

The Municipal Treasurer ensures to determine the current tax receivable based on a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the corresponding amounts due and collectible and furnish this to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis for preparing the required journal entries to reflect the corrected amount of Special Education Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax.

The Municipal Accountant ensures to record only checks that are released to claimants pursuant to Section 43 of the NGAs Manual Volume 1. Thus, the Municipal Accountant ensures to draw the proposed Journal Entry Voucher to adjust for the checks that are unreleased to claimants at year-end.

Management ensures to cause the closure of the DBP payroll account since the purpose of opening this account is no longer being fulfilled and transfer the remaining balance to an active bank account of the General Fund.

Management ensures to direct the responsible personnel to settle the penalty resulting from the late remittance of PhilHealth contributions, ensuring that government funds are not utilized for this purpose. Additionally, management ensures consistent and timely remittance of contributions to avoid imposition of interest and penalties and compromised availment of intended benefits.

The Municipal Engineer ensures to place a signboard on the foregoing project and to submit a picture of the signboard, already with the complete and appropriate data, for further evaluation and re-inspection by the Audit Team.

The Municipal Agriculturist ensures to regularly monitor the changes and movement of Biological Assets to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account. Likewise, management ensures to conduct a physical count on these assets and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories/ Biological Assets.
Management ensures to submit the Schedule of Requirements and Price Schedules on the foregoing listed disbursements of the municipality for evaluation by the audit team.

Management ensures to specifically indicate in the Annual Budget the programs and activities to be undertaken by the LGU in order to facilitate proper charging and monitoring of limited, public funds.

Management ensures to submit a copy of the Resolution authorizing the municipality to deposit funds with FCB and secure a written approval from the Executive Director of the BLGF for the purpose. Otherwise, the Local Chief Executive ensures to propose to the SB council to effect closure of the FCB bank account and maintain their funds only with Authorized Government Depository Banks.

The Local Chief Executive ensures to require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible, including the current list of delinquent taxpayers, to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2023.
Management ensures that the municipality will issue a letter to the supplier demanding payment of the computed liquidated damages.

Management ensures that the municipality and beneficiary associations will draft and sign a Memorandum of Agreement stating therein the conditions and duties of both parties for the proper and efficient use of the properties enumerated, and submit an original copy of the document to the COA auditor assigned.
observations.reason: Due to the occurrence of a fortuitous event, as substantiated in a letter submitted by the Municipal Agriculturist.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive ensures to require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for CY 2019 and the ensuing year, including current list of delinquent taxpayers to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2019. The Municipal Accountant ensures to prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to correct the RPT and SET Receivables being set-up for years 2019 and 2020. The Municipal Accountant ensures to reconcile the difference of the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables from that of the supposed balance as per computation presented.

The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, ensures to take the necessary steps and process the release of shares to the appropriate recipients like the provincial government and the barangays for their share of realty tax, community tax and others which were not yet released for several years now.
observations.reason: Checks for barangay releases were prepared, and the recommendation for the Provincial Government’s shares was closed due to the previous Municipal Treasurer’s non-remittance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the balances of cash accounts in their respective books in order to arrive at accurate and reliable account balances and present fair and accurate financial statements. 

Likewise, identify the items that need adjustment and record them appropriately in the affected books.  

Henceforth, we recommended that regular reconciliation of accounts be done, at least quarterly as prescribed, so that differences would be immediately corrected.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible, including the current list of delinquent taxpayers, to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2023.

We recommended that management to require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same Circular.

We recommended that Management follow-up and monitor the reconciliation of the account balances of the various Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts by the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer, General Services Officer, Property Custodian and other concerned personnel. 

Furthermore, we recommended that management adhere and follow COA Circular 2020-006 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of the municipality.

We recommended that management complete the necessary documentary requirements in order to fully liquidate funds received from the various National Government Agencies and return all unused/excess funds pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013. 

Should the management consider using the unutilized funds, we recommended that prior approval be sought from the source agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record office supplies purchased as part of inventory and only recognize expense upon issuances thereof. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO maintain SLCs and Stock and Property Cards respectively in accordance to Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that the municipality issue a letter to the supplier demanding payment of the computed liquidated damages.

We recommended that the municipality and beneficiary associations draft and sign a Memorandum of Agreement stating therein the conditions and duties of both parties for the proper and efficient use of the properties enumerated, and submit an original copy of the document to the COA auditor assigned.

We recommended that management require the Head of the Inventory Committee to place property stickers on all identified properties of the LGU which will contain vital information suggested on Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that the municipality exercise fully the revenue collecting power vested on them as provided in Sections 142 to 150 of R.A. 7160 to increase its income to finance projects for the constituents and not be dependent with the Internal Revenue Allotment from the national government.

We recommended that management make proper and immediate representation with the concerned source agencies to confirm balances of funds transferred to the LGU and start completing the necessary documentary requirements in order to fully liquidate funds received and return all unused/excess funds pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013. 

Should the management consider using the unutilized funds, we recommended that management communicate such request to the source agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for CY 2019 and the ensuing year, including current list of delinquent taxpayers to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2019.  

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to correct the RPT and SET Receivables being set-up for years 2019 and 2020.   

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference of the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables from that of the supposed balance as per computation presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify revenue collection and issue Notices of Delinquency in accordance with the procedures of Section 254 of RA 7160 to inform owners of real property units who are delinquent in the payment of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, take the necessary steps and process the release of shares to the appropriate recipients like the provincial government and the barangays for their share of realty tax, community tax and others which were not yet released for several years now.

We recommended that management send demand letters for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances. 

We further recommended that, when applicable, management initiate the filing of request for the write-off of dormant receivable accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that management constitute a committee to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties in the manner provided for in Section 79 of PD 1445 and for the Municipal Accountant to effect the appropriate entries to drop the value of the same from the books after disposal.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO or the Municipal Treasurer to cause the renewal of all AREs once every three (3) years or whenever there is a change in the custodianship of municipal properties. 
 
Further, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to go over all of the properties of the LGU and see to it that each one is appropriately covered by an ARE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to give focus on the task of preparing the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant & Equipment (RPCPPE) and comply with the provision of Section 124 of the NGAS.  

Moreover, require the reconciliation of records and effect the necessary adjustments in case of deficiencies and instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately effect the adjustments of the discrepancies between the actual physical count and the recorded balances of the affected Inventory and Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

We recommended that Management initiate action for the recovery of the amount embezzled.  

Verify with the Municipal Assessor if the concerned accountable officer has property in his name and exercise lien over his estate in satisfaction of his indebtedness. 

In such situations, direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of any money due from persons concerned to be applied in satisfaction of their indebtedness.
Require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare documents to transfer ownership and record of these PPE items to the barangays. Subsequently, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare adjusting entries to rectify the overstatement of the LGU’s asset, contra-asset, and expense accounts.

We suggested and Management agreed that the following journal entry be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry – Books of the LGU:
To record transfer of PPEs to appropriate barangays.

We recommended and Management agreed to require both the Accounting and Treasury Departments to conduct the immediate reconciliation of accounts to correct the noted differences and that at least quarterly reconciliation of the balances in the cashbook and ledgers be done to ensure that the reported funds are accurate and reliable.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the appropriate funds of the 20 per cent development fund to its designated bank account and monitor its use to avoid wastage or loss of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer determine the current tax receivable based on a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the corresponding amounts due and collectible and furnish this to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis for preparing the required journal entries to reflect the corrected amount of Special Education Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record only checks that are released to claimants pursuant to Section 43 of the NGAs Manual Volume 1. Thus, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the following proposed Journal Entry Voucher to adjust for the checks that are unreleased to claimants at year-end:

We recommended that the Inventory Committee prioritize the conduct of complete inventory count of all its Property, Plant and Equipment in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 to support the existence, completeness and valuation of the PPE of the municipality and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team concerned.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the closure of the DBP payroll account since the purpose of opening this account is no longer being fulfilled and transfer the remaining balance to an active bank account of the General Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the responsible personnel to settle the penalty resulting from the late remittance of PhilHealth contributions, ensuring that government funds are not utilized for this purpose. Additionally, we recommended consistent and timely remittance of contributions to avoid imposition of interest and penalties and compromised availment of intended benefits.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer place a signboard on the foregoing project and to submit a picture of the signboard, already with the complete and appropriate data, for further evaluation and re-inspection by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist regularly monitor the changes and movement of Biological Assets to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account. Likewise, conduct a physical count on these assets and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories/Biological Assets.

We recommended that Management consistently implement proper waste segregation as prescribed in RA 9003 and maintain order around the vicinity of its MRF to promote public health and care for the environment.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Schedule of Requirements and Price Schedules on the foregoing listed disbursements of the municipality for evaluation by the audit team.

We recommended that Management specifically indicate in the Annual Budget the programs and activities to be undertaken by the LGU in order to facilitate proper charging and monitoring of limited, public funds.

We recommended and management agreed to submit a copy of the Resolution authorizing the municipality to deposit funds with FCB and secure a written approval from the Executive Director of the BLGF for the purpose. Otherwise, the Local Chief Executive propose to the SB council to effect closure of the FCB bank account and maintain their funds only with Authorized Government Depository Banks.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the reports and charges to the LDRRM Fund to find out the cause of the difference and make necessary adjustment where appropriate.

We recommended that management stop granting cash advances to the Disbursing Officer (DO) in excess of the maximum cash accountability or evaluate if there is a need to increase the DO’s amount of bond to cover higher cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of pre-signing checks and only affix their signatures on checks which are duly supported with approved Disbursement Vouchers to prevent loss and improper disbursement of government funds.
We recommended that the municipality submit a written explanation on its nonconformance to the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended the following PAJE:
	
        (dr) Prior Period Adjustment	 2,005,350.00
         (cr) Due to NGAs 2,005,350.00
	
We further recommended that the municipality debit only the Due to NGAs account after submission of the liquidation reports on the completed disbursement to DOLE.

We recommended the following PAJE:

        (dr) Prior Period Adjustment    1,327,000.00
        (cr) Due to NGAs 1,327,000.00

We further recommended that henceforth, the municipality debit only the Due to NGAs account after submission of the liquidation reports on the completed procurements to the DOLE, as well as other source agencies as stipulated in the applicable MOA.

We recommended that the municipality stop procurement from any entity whose officials are at the same time connected with the LGU’s Procurement Committee such as the GARCBC as well as those that have influence in the process. 

Justification from the employees indicated earlier on the noted deficiency is recommended to be submitted for evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that management prioritize the implementation of the continuing projects of the 20% development fund.  

Further, projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the constantly escalating prices of materials.

We recommended that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved.

We recommend that management require the Head of the Inventory Committee to place property stickers on all identified properties of the LGU which will contain vital information suggested on Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: In the absence of the Inventory Committee Head during the Exit Conference, the Municipal Accountant assured that they will comply with the new circular intended for the PPEs of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for CY 2019 and the ensuing year, including current list of delinquent taxpayers to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2019.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to correct the RPT and SET Receivables being set-up for years 2019 and 2020.   Further, we recommend  that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference of the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables from that of the supposed balance as per computation presented.
observations.reason: Verification from the Municipal Accountant revealed that the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer have not furnished the lists. Thus, adjustments could not be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer intensify revenue collection and issue Notices of Delinquency in accordance with the procedures of Section 254 of RA 7160 to inform owners of real property units who are delinquent in the payment of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.
observations.reason: Verification thru audit of the 2021 Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send demand letters for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances. We further recommend that, when applicable, management initiate the filing of request for the write-off of dormant receivable accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Some of these amounts have already been paid and some of the debtors are already deceased.
observations.reason: No proof that demand letters were sent and request for write-off was not yet forwarded to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management follow-up and monitor the reconciliation of the account balances of the various Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts by the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer, General Services Officer, Property Custodian and other concerned personnel, giving special attention to the resolution of the unreconciled balance under Other Machinery & Equipment account.
observations.reason: These properties are still booked per verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner and avoid splitting of government contracts.
observations.managementAction: The said practice was stopped.
observations.reason: Verification thru audit of the 2021 disbursement vouchers revealed that splitting is still done.

observations.recommendation: Require the immediate refund from all concerned officials and employees who receive the   PEI the amounts in excess of the ₱10,000.00 authorized in the DBM Circular. Henceforth, stop the practice on disbursement of funds without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: Order refund of PEI the amount in excess of the ₱10,000.00.
observations.reason: Refund was not yet made on the amounts received in excess of ₱10,000.00.

Further, if circumstances warrant, please facilitate adjustment in the payment of benefits among frontliners that those underpaid be paid the amount due them and those overpaid be required to refund the overpayment.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer justified that they were just using the default format of Purchase Orders which do not contain any warranty clause especially during emergency cases. observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the NOA has been posted to the PhilGEPS Website but not on the GPPB Portal.

Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the Standard Retail Price (SRP) set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO explained that during the procurement of these items, no suppliers offered prices compliant with the stated DOH Memorandum, thus leaving them with no choice but to procure such items above the SRPs and expedite the procurement due to the COVID-19 outbreak.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.
observations.managementAction: Management excluded the income from the local economic enterprise resulting to its deficiency. Revision on the GAD plan for 2020 will be done in order to comply with the minimum requirement.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify as non-current assets, the portion of receivables which do not conform to the criteria provided for in paragraph 76 of the IPSAS, in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management follow-up and monitor the reconciliation of the account balances of the various Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts by the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer, General Services Officer, Property Custodian and other concerned personnel, giving special attention to the resolution of the unreconciled balance under Other Machinery & Equipment account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting Department prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed projects still recorded under Construction in Progress – Agency Asset accounts into their appropriate asset accounts and take up depreciation so that the correct balances will be reported in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management revert the unexpended balance of CYs 2011-2013 LDRRMF appropriation back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the local sanggunian for other social services.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management constitute a committee to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties in the manner provided for in Section 79 of PD 1445 and for the Municipal Accountant to effect the appropriate entries to drop the value of the same from the books after disposal.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis.  Henceforth, we recommend that reciprocal accounts be checked such that upon closing of the books at year-end, no balances would remain in the consolidated financial statements.

Recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management maximize the services of permanent employees and minimize the hiring of casual, contractual and job order employees as to necessity in order to observe and adhere with the austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.

observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials shall verify if the accountable officer still has property in his name and exercise lien over his estate in satisfaction of his indebtedness.
observations.reason: Accordingly, there are no more properties under the name of the previous Municipal Treasurer. Processing of claim of his retirement benefits are also still on-going as they have difficulties securing the signatures of the heirs. This should be given priority so that this account shall be settled.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to update the list of delinquent Real Property taxpayers so that they can identify and determine the amount of taxes still due and collectible and make constant follow-up. This will also aid management in formulating strategies to enforce payment such as applying the remedies in Section 254 of R.A. 7160 to maximize revenues. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible as basis for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special education tax receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The list of delinquent Real Property Taxpayers is used as basis in setting up of the real property tax receivable/special education tax receivable.

observations.recommendation: The implementers should carefully/properly plan, conduct feasibility study where necessary, before a project will be implemented/ administered in order to utilize funds economically, to ensure efficient output at a desired level of performance, and to speed up completion of the project that would improve the socio-economic development of the locality. The Local Officials should come up with a concrete decision in order to maximize the utilization of the purchased lots to avoid wastage of government funds. Project implementers should observe pertinent rules and comply with the requirements before pursuing the proposed municipal cemetery to avoid any protests and or court litigations. All projects included in the Annual Investment Plan should be implemented within the time frame set. The Local Officials should come up with a concrete decision in order to maximize the utilization of the purchased lots to avoid wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials are directed to secure the necessary permits and licenses from appropriate agencies to support the implementation of the project.
observations.reason: The Municipal Officials are awaiting for all necessary permits before they will start with the project.

observations.recommendation: Management: Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate accounts – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Chart of Accounts. Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 45% Personal Services limitation and to minimize the hiring of casuals as to the necessity. Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is directed to record the wages of casual employees to their appropriate accounts.
observations.reason: Management still has to review the personnel complement of every department and the extent of their functions so that necessity of hiring job order can be determined.

observations.recommendation: Require the accounting/ treasury sections to verify the origin of these accounts and when pertinent documents are available send proper bills to debtors for its collection. Pursue continued collection, otherwise, request for write-off of the accounts from the Central Office of the Commission on Audit when collectibility is nil, supported with the required documents. Require the municipal accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality after the result of the verification and request for write-off when collectability is nil to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Accordingly, efforts were done to locate the original documents as basis for adjustments. Collection demand /bills could not be facilitated unless the original documents are retrieved. Contemplating to request for write-off Could not be acted upon unless the above documents are located.
observations.reason: Some of the receivable s were already adjusted; Still in the process of locating the documents to support the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management that all insurances of the vehicles and properties of the municipality should be with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) to safeguard the loss of government resources due to damage/accidents and calamities. Henceforth, effective immediately insured all properties of the Local Government Unit with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: According to management. they had already insured some LGU vehicles with private insurance company
observations.reason: Two (2) vehicles purchased during the conduct of LDAP-RPTA were still insured with private insurance companies. The LGU was having a hard time insuring it with the GSIS as they were asking for the vouchers which were among those damaged during the previous typhoons that hit the municipality

observations.recommendation: Require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

observations.reason: On the process of titling.
